data.content:
Implementation review rejected.

Findings:
1. `src/lib/terminal-manager-take-child.ts:146-165` still routes interactive Gemini take-loop sessions through the generic fallback argv `-p --input-format stream-json --verbose --output-format stream-json --dangerously-skip-permissions`; the reviewed commit only adds Gemini-specific spawn handling to claimed-step path in `src/lib/terminal-manager-initial-child.ts:334-339`. Acceptance criterion 1 therefore still fails for take-loop sessions.
2. `src/lib/agent-adapter.ts:95-113`, `src/lib/agent-session-runtime.ts:180-186`, `src/lib/terminal-manager-format.ts:76-83`, and `src/lib/__tests__/gemini-interactive-session.test.ts:250-269` define Gemini interactive mode as `gemini --sandbox -o stream-json` plus stdin `{"type":"user_turn"...}` writes. But the installed Gemini CLI v0.36.0 local help and bundled docs document `--output-format stream-json` for non-interactive scripting, state that `--prompt-interactive` cannot be used when piping stdin, and expose `--acp` as the explicit protocol mode. This implementation still depends on an undocumented stdin/session contract rather than a verified Gemini interactive/session surface, so acceptance criteria 1-4 are not met.
3. Sanity gates on detached artifact `82cff361673ca5f713731a498229978dfa29b02a` passed in `/tmp/foolery-54a2-review-82cff-22543`: `bun run lint` (1 existing React Compiler warning in `src/components/beat-form.tsx`), `bunx tsc --noEmit`, `bun run test` (228 files passed), and `bun run build`. The rejection is correctness/spec compliance, not CI.
